rulings.Case Title: STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN ET AL VS. SALMAN SHARIAT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2025 line 1. PETITIONER STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN, JULIA CHOPE MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that this petition is the proper mechanism for an "action in civil court to collect the amount owed" referenced in the Rent Board decision. Moving party also fails to provide any authority for the standard of review or any requirements for the moving party to prevail in the "civil action" contemplated by the Rent Board decision. The following argument in the Points and Authorities is not followed by a citation to a statute, ordinance or case law: ""To prevail, Tenants need only provide sufficient evidence to show that 1) the Rent Board issued a Decision ordering Landlord to make payment to Tenants in the amount now requested; 2) the Rent Board's decision is final; and 3) Landlord has not made payment to Tenants." (Moving Points and Authorities 5:17-20). Unauthorized supplemental opposition and reply filed months after the original hearing date without leave of Court are stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMIE EDSON ET AL VS. AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC., AIRBNB, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (in line with the cases cited in opposition) giving rise to public injunctive relief. Plaintiff shall also allege facts showing that Defendant, under the circumstances of this case, including relevant contracts terms and disclosure contained therein, is subject to escrow laws. Plaintiff shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CUSTOM CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES VS. JAMES LODUCA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment / Summary Adjudication As To Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2025 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT BLOOM AND LAWN LANDSCAPE INC , A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment / Summary Adjudication As To Cross-Complaint is OFF CALENDAR. First Amended Cross-Complaint filed on January 22, 2025. "After a cause of action is amended, the court may rule in favor of the defendant if, upon subsequent motion, or perhaps renewal of the earlier motion if appropriately framed, it is shown ... there are no triable material issues of fact which would permit recovery on that theory." State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JALASAI BAPU, LLC VS. RAMON MARTINEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court'S Entry Of Defendant'S Default, And Court Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2025 line 5. PLAINTIFF JALASAI BAPU, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court'S Entry Of Defendant'S Default, And Court Judgment is DENIED. Plaintiff cites no authority and provides no argument for the proposition that Plaintiff should not allege compliance with or exemption from TPA 2019, which governs eviction in the state of California, an allegation the Judicial Council specifically included in the form UD complaint. Plaintiff improperly relies on a non-citable trial Court decision of which Plaintiff seeks judicial notice. Per the Request for judicial notice Plainitffs seeks notice of 3 filings in case CUD-28-673580. No such case exists. To the extent P meant to ask for judicial notice of case CUD-23-673580, Plaintiff does not explain how the Court overruling a demurrer based on uncertainty (re: failure to allege whether or not unit falls under the Tenant Protection Action of 2019) makes it proper to enter a court (or clerk) default on a complaint that does not allege compliance with or exemption from TPA 2019. The Court notes that Plaintiff fails to point to any allegations in the Complaint from which the Court may make an inference that the eviction is in compliance with the just causes listed in TPA 2019 or is exempt from just cause eviction provisions of TPA 2019. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required.
